name: Toni Tassani
title: VP of Engineering
summary: Engineering leader at the intersection of technical excellence and human dynamics. Over 25 years spanning software development, architecture, Agile coaching, and senior leadership. At Ocado Technology, built and led an Engineering Practices function of ~25 people — including Application Security (~15), Engineering Practice Leads, and Agile Coaching — driving Engineering Productivity and Developer Experience across 2,500 technologists in 8 locations. Energised by sociotechnical challenges — coaching individuals, facilitating change, and designing organisations that reduce friction and improve outcomes.

experience.role: VP of Engineering Practices and Application Security
experience.desc: Ocado Technology provides the Ocado Smart Platform (OSP)—an end-to-end automation and e-commerce solution—to 13 international retail partners. ~2,500 technologists across 8 locations; 3,000+ patents in AI-driven logistics.
bullets: Reporting to the CTO; part of the Engineering Productivity stream
Identifying best engineering practices and extending them across the organisation
Responsible for Application Security, Lean and Agile, and the SDLC
Leading adoption of **Gen AI for Software Engineering** (trials, metrics, alignment, enablement)
Leading the Application Security function (~20 people), 4 Engineering Practice Leads, an Accessibility expert and an Agile Coach
Measuring proxies to Engineering Productivity and Developer Experience, qualitatively and quantitatively
Working with Cloud teams and SRE to find finance optimisations in cloud consumption
Supervising compliance and cost of applications and processes for SOC 2
Continued leading Engineering Practices and Application Security with expanded organisational remit
Built the Engineering Practices function from the ground up, partnering with the Deputy CEngO for Technology to promote software architecture and delivery excellence across the organisation
Short-term assignment to establish an SRE function within Platform Services in Barcelona, before transitioning to the Engineering Practices stream
Member of the Catalysts team, around 10 people, focusing on Culture, Effectiveness and Efficiency using Agile and Lean tools

experience.desc: Online Travel Agency with 1,700 employees, 400+ in IT, headquartered in Barcelona.
bullets: Led Agile transformation with a group of 10 coaches; using Kanban with support from David J. Anderson
Rolled out to 50+ development teams; coached 14 teams (max 4 at a time)
Applied Kanban in non-IT teams: Learning & Development and Help Desk
Taught Kanban, Social Styles, effective feedback, visual communication, and other topics
Supported the DevOps group continuous improvement; navigated team conflicts; coached individuals
Re-organisational design with Dev Managers; kick-start with 80 participants (McKinsey support)
Built a Kanban maturity visualisation tool using JavaScript / D3.js
Conducted Impact Mapping sessions with POs; identified Value Streams; presented agile plan to C-suite
Teaching Agile Methodologies to international postgraduate students in the Master in Project Management, covering both software and non-software contexts
Contributed to software architecture evolution and Continuous Delivery improvements for La Caixa (banking) and UOC (university)
Software architecture, Continuous Delivery and API Management
Supported one team in improving Agile processes and technical practices

experience.company: Thomson Reuters Life Sciences (now Clarivate Analytics)
experience.desc: Thomson Reuters manages information for professionals. The Life Sciences division managed data useful for drug design in the Pharma industry such as clinical trials, regulatory information, and scientific literature.
bullets: Developed and deployed Cortellis; progressed from Lead Senior Analyst Programmer to Solutions Architect
Built data APIs, Web Services and Applications in Java and Scala
Collaborated with emerging DevOps department; facilitated meetings; trained distributed teams
Transitioned methodologies to DSDM Atern and Scrum
Consultant for Thomson Reuters Barcelona following the acquisition of Prous Science
Trained the development team in modern practices and Java; helped with Agile transition
Assisted in building a web application for Pharma scientists
Development Manager and Architect; team of 15 across Barcelona and Madrid
Set up new Java development department; built Primary Care management web app for Madrid
Part-time Lecturer in database subjects at the Open University (distance learning)
Developed and led project delivery across multiple client sites in Spain, using Oracle, Java, and Web technologies. Managed teams of up to 5 people.

education.degree: Postgraduate in Agile Methods for Product Development (PMA)
education.school: BES La Salle – Universitat Ramon Llull
